check: Verify screenshot failure path stays consistent with server retry semantics
finding: When a render fails, screenshot.py renames the source HTML to .failed and stops scanning it, but the server writes the screenshot row back to status='pending' for retry_count < 3. That leaves a pending screenshot with no remaining producer to retry it, so screenshot progress can remain stuck indefinitely after the first failure.

check: Verify failed HTML is actually preserved for debugging
finding: The new .failed rename no longer blocks batch completion, but _check_batch_completion() immediately removes the whole batch directory once no .html files remain. In practice the .failed artifact is deleted right away, so the promised postmortem HTML is not retained.

check: Run lightweight syntax validation on screenshot worker changes
